hypothesis: Combining transfer learning strategies sequentially—where reinforcement learning is applied to a model first fine-tuned on domain-specific data rather than on a pre-trained general model—will produce higher percentages of molecules passing material-specific filters than the currently optimal fine-tuning plus reinforcement learning approach, because the intermediate fine-tuning step creates a more stable reward landscape that prevents policy collapse and reduces the exploration-exploitation trade-off that currently causes the observed negative correlation between diversity and filter-passing metrics.
